completed_slice.finding: Epistemic class and lifecycle transition must be represented separately. Durable claims remain immutable; accepted, challenged, narrowed, superseded, and resolved are typed edges that preserve both old and new claims.
completed_slice.verification: Artifact committed through the GitHub contents API; fetch-back verification pending in this run.

observed: CM-1022 preserves source-class precedence and CM-1023 qualifies negative evidence by inspected scope.
Prior continuity artifacts lacked a reusable claim-level transition contract.
inferred: Explicit transition edges are required to preserve contradiction and language-evolution history during effective-state reconciliation.
proposed: Implement a validator and reconciler for claim and transition graphs using the CM-1024 contract.
superseded: Last-write-wins mutation of claim text or one overloaded status field.
unresolved: Legacy continuity claims need stable claim IDs before full graph reconciliation.
Origins for M-004, M-005, and M-006 remain unresolved.

remaining: Create a JSON Schema for claim nodes and transition edges.
Implement deterministic validation for dangling references, cycles, authority violations, and unsupported scope expansion.
Backfill stable claim IDs into a non-destructive mapping artifact rather than rewriting prior records.
Complete source-manifest enumeration before making exhaustive absence claims.
privacy_boundary: Repository metadata and abstract continuity rules only; no private user material was retained.
